text.value: The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: ' A patient-specific ordered anti-cancer therapy regimen instance. This resource is included in the CDS Hooks draftOrders Bundle and referenced in context.selections at order-select and order-sign. RequestGroup.instantiatesCanonical SHALL be populated with the canonical URL of the AntiCancerRegimenPlanDefinition when the canonical regimen definition is known. Two scheduling patterns are supported in regimen actions: Cycle-day timing — Each action (or action.action for phased regimens) uses the local extension http://hl7.org/fhir/us/codex-ocpa/StructureDefinition/regimen-days-of-cycle on action.timingTiming to declare which days of the cycle the drug is administered. The action.timingTiming.repeat carries the machine-computable cycle period. Sequential phase ordering — For multi-phase regimens (e.g., AC→T), top-level action groups represent phases and action.relatedAction with relationship = after-end declares that the second phase begins after the first. mCODE Migration Candidate — This profile is proposed for inclusion in mCODE STU5. It is the MVP artifact for oncology prior authorization: the selected clinical unit passed to the CRD service. '

The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: ' An Observation that documents the ordinal line of systemic anti-cancer therapy a patient is currently receiving or has received for a given cancer diagnosis. This observation is a required patient data element for oncology prior authorization: most metastatic and recurrent cancer regimen policies are line-of-therapy dependent. Observation.code uses the local OGCA code system code line-of-therapy as a placeholder. A migration request for a LOINC code will be submitted before mCODE STU5. Observation.value[x] SHALL be a CodeableConcept drawn from TreatmentLineVS. The focus SHALL reference the primary cancer Condition for which this line applies. mCODE Migration Candidate — Proposed for inclusion in mCODE STU5. '

The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'For CDS Clients claiming conformance to this oncology CRD profile, when an anti-cancer therapy regimen is selected or signed, the client SHALL include the oncology CRD extension in the CDS Hooks request.'

The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'The key words SHALL, SHOULD, MAY, and SHALL NOT in this specification are to be interpreted as described in RFC 2119.'

The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'All cancer-specific Library instances SHALL declare requirements covering these categories where applicable:'

The html source contains the word 'MAY' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'instantiatesCanonical (Must Support) — canonical URL of the PlanDefinition being ordered, when a published definition exists; MAY be omitted when the regimen originates from a local order-set without a canonical definition'

The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'The EHR SHALL have access to relevant patient context (mCODE-based Observations, Conditions, MedicationRequests)'
The html source contains the word 'MAY' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: '// Lists each supported cancer type Library. An OGCA-aware EHR MAY use this'
The IG is not for the international realm, and it uses SNOMED CT, so it should fix the SCT edition in the expansion parameters
Reference to experimental CodeSystem http://hl7.org/fhir/us/codex-ocpa/CodeSystem/treatment-line-cs|0.1.0
Reference to experimental CodeSystem http://hl7.org/fhir/us/codex-ocpa/CodeSystem/treatment-line-cs|0.1.0
A definition for CodeSystem 'http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm' version '05062024' could not be found, so the code cannot be validated. Valid versions: 03022026
A definition for CodeSystem 'http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm' version '05062024' could not be found, so the code cannot be validated. Valid versions: 03022026

There are multiple different potential matches for the url 'http://hl7.org/fhir/us/davinci-crd/ImplementationGuide/hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd'. It might be a good idea to fix to the correct version to reduce the likelihood of a wrong version being selected by an implementation/implementer, or use the [IG Parameter `pin-canonicals`](https://hl7.org/fhir/tools/CodeSystem-ig-parameters.html). Using version '2.2.1', found versions: 2.0.0 (from hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd#2.0.0), 2.1.0 (from hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd#2.1.0), 2.2.1 (from hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd#current). Suggested fix: change the canonical reference from 'http://hl7.org/fhir/us/davinci-crd/ImplementationGuide/hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd' to 'http://hl7.org/fhir/us/davinci-crd/ImplementationGuide/hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd|2.2.1'

There are multiple different potential matches for the url 'http://hl7.org/fhir/us/davinci-crd/ImplementationGuide/hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd'. It might be a good idea to fix to the correct version to reduce the likelihood of a wrong version being selected by an implementation/implementer, or use the [IG Parameter `pin-canonicals`](https://hl7.org/fhir/tools/CodeSystem-ig-parameters.html). Using version '2.2.1', found versions: 2.0.0 (from hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd#2.0.0), 2.1.0 (from hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd#2.1.0), 2.2.1 (from hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd#current). Suggested fix: change the canonical reference from 'http://hl7.org/fhir/us/davinci-crd/ImplementationGuide/hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd' to 'http://hl7.org/fhir/us/davinci-crd/ImplementationGuide/hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd|2.2.1'

Most code systems defined in HL7 IGs will need to move to THO later during the process. Consider giving this code system a THO URL now (See https://confluence.hl7.org/display/TSMG/Terminology+Play+Book, and/or talk to TSMG)

valueCode.value: Resource is not deprecated, but the description mentions deprecated - check whether it should be deprecated

text.value: Resource is not deprecated, but the description mentions deprecated - check whether it should be deprecated

Most code systems defined in HL7 IGs will need to move to THO later during the process. Consider giving this code system a THO URL now (See https://confluence.hl7.org/display/TSMG/Terminology+Play+Book, and/or talk to TSMG)
Reference to draft CodeSystem http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|0.5.0
Reference to draft CodeSystem http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|0.5.0

The ImplementationGuide uses package hl7.fhir.uv.extensions.r4#5.2.0 released on 2025-02-10, but the most recent appropriate version is 5.3.0. This reference is getting old and the more recent version should be considered

The ImplementationGuide uses package hl7.fhir.us.core#7.0.0 released on 2024-05-08, but the most recent appropriate version is 8.0.1. This reference is getting old and the more recent version should be considered

The ImplementationGuide uses package hl7.fhir.us.davinci-crd#2.1.0 released on 2024-12-11, but the most recent appropriate version is 2.2.1. This reference is getting old and the more recent version should be considered

The ImplementationGuide uses package hl7.fhir.us.davinci-dtr#2.0.1 released on 2024-01-11, but the most recent appropriate version is 3.0.1. This reference is getting old and the more recent version should be considered

The resource Library/BreastCancerPADataRequirements could usefully have an OID assigned (OIDs are easy to assign - see https://build.fhir.org/ig/FHIR/fhir-tools-ig/CodeSystem-ig-parameters.html#ig-parameters-auto-oid-root)

There are multiple different potential matches for the url 'http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/StructureDefinition/us-core-patient'. It might be a good idea to fix to the correct version to reduce the likelihood of a wrong version being selected by an implementation/implementer, or use the [IG Parameter `pin-canonicals`](https://hl7.org/fhir/tools/CodeSystem-ig-parameters.html). Using version '7.0.0', found versions: 3.1.1 (from hl7.fhir.us.core#3.1.1), 6.1.0 (from hl7.fhir.us.core#6.1.0), 7.0.0 (from hl7.fhir.us.core#7.0.0). Suggested fix: change the canonical reference from 'http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/StructureDefinition/us-core-patient' to 'http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/StructureDefinition/us-core-patient|7.0.0'
Reference to experimental CodeSystem http://hl7.org/fhir/us/codex-ocpa/CodeSystem/treatment-line-cs|0.1.0

The repeating element has a pattern. The pattern will apply to all the repeats (this has not been clear to all users)
US FHIR Usage rules require that all profiles on Observation derive from one of the base US profiles. See https://confluence.hl7.org/display/CGP/US+Core+Variance+Request+Process
